---
title: "Publications"
description:
site: distill::distill_website
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

My [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=zhIGL70AAAAJ&hl=en) and [ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Pin-Chun-Chen-6) profiles.

*Names in **bold** indicate my authorship; \* denotes co-first authors.*

### Under Review

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Stritzelberger, J., Hamer, H., & Staresina, B. P. (2026). Hippocampal ripples evoke a stereotyped cortical response followed by spindle-mediated network synchronization. *bioRxiv*. [https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.05.14.725103](https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.05.14.725103)

### Original Research Articles

- Zhang, J.\*, **Chen, P.-C.**\*, Mednick, S. C., & Tambini, A. (in press). Slow oscillations modulate functional brain changes supporting working memory. *Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience*.

- Subramoniam, A., **Chen, P.-C.**, Sattari, N., Mednick, S., & Whitehurst, L. (in press). Sleep dependent autonomic correlates of affect and arousal in younger and older adults. *Sleep Advances*.

- Jacobsen, S. S., Morehouse, A. B., **Chen, P.-C.**, Gomolka, R. S., Andersen, M., Nedergaard, M., Mednick, S. C., & Kjaerby, C. (2026). Noradrenergic infraslow rhythm during sleep is the critical link between heart-rate dynamics and memory consolidation. *eLife*. [Link](https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.110252.1)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Stritzelberger, J., Walther, K., Hamer, H., & Staresina, B. (2025). Hippocampal ripples during offline periods predict motor sequence learning. *Journal of Neuroscience*. [Link](https://www.jneurosci.org/content/early/2025/10/13/JNEUROSCI.1502-25.2025.long)

- Morehouse, A., Simon, K., **Chen, P.-C.**, & Mednick, S. C. (2025). Heart rate variability during REM sleep is associated with reduced negative memory bias. *Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience*. [Link](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/behavioral-neuroscience/articles/10.3389/fnbeh.2025.1513655/full)

- Shuster, A., **Chen, P.-C.**, Niknazar, H., McDevitt, E., Lopour, B., & Mednick, S. (2024). Novel electrophysiological signatures of learning and forgetting in human REM sleep. *Journal of Neuroscience*. [Link](https://github.com/pinchunc/papers/blob/main/jneuro_NovelElectrophysiologicalREM.pdf)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Simon, K. C., Sattari, N., Whitehurst, L. N., Naji, M., & Mednick, S. C. (2022). Autonomic-central coupling during daytime sleep differs between older and younger people. *Neurobiology of Learning and Memory*, 107646. [Link](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1074742722000703)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Niknazar, H., Alaynick, W. A., Whitehurst, L. N., & Mednick, S. C. (2021). Competitive dynamics underlie cognitive improvements during sleep. *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences*, 118(51), e2109339118. [Link](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2109339118)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Sattari, N., Whitehurst, L. N., & Mednick, S. C. (2021). Age-related losses in cardiac autonomic activity during a daytime nap. *Psychophysiology*, 58(7), e13701. [Link](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/psyp.13701)

- **Chen, P.-C.**\*, Whitehurst, L. N.\*, Naji, M., & Mednick, S. C. (2020). Autonomic activity during a daytime nap facilitates working memory improvement. *Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience*, 32(10), 1963–1974. [Link](https://github.com/pinchunc/papers/blob/main/jocn_AutonomicActivityduringaDaytimeNap.pdf)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Whitehurst, L. N., Naji, M., & Mednick, S. C. (2020). Autonomic/central coupling benefits working memory in healthy young adults. *Neurobiology of Learning and Memory*, 173, 107267. [Link](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1074742720301118)

### Reviews & Perspectives

- Shuster, A. E., Morehouse, A., McDevitt, E. A., **Chen, P.-C.**, Whitehurst, L. N., Zhang, J., Sattari, N., Uzoigwe, T., Ekhlasi, A., Cai, D., Simon, K., Niethard, N., & Mednick, S. C. (2025). REM refines and rescues memory representations: A new theory. *Sleep Advances*, 6(1), zpaf004. [Link](https://academic.oup.com/sleepadvances/article/6/1/zpaf004/7972492)

- **Chen, P.-C.**, Zhang, J., Thayer, J. F., & Mednick, S. C. (2022). Understanding the roles of central and autonomic activity during sleep in the improvement of working memory and episodic memory. *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences*. [Link](https://www.pnas.org/doi/full/10.1073/pnas.2123417119)

- Whitehurst, L. N., **Chen, P.-C.**, Naji, M., & Mednick, S. C. (2020). New directions in sleep and memory research: The role of autonomic activity. *Current Opinion in Behavioral Sciences*, 33, 17–24. [Link](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S235215461930110X)

### Other Publications

*Publications outside my main research area, from earlier collaborative work.*

- Kim, D. J., Yuan, T.-A., **Chen, P.-C.**, Liu-Smith, F., Atanaskova Mesinkovska, N., & Grande Sarpa, H. (2021). Pediatric melanoma in the Hispanic population: An analysis of institutional and national data. *Pediatric Dermatology*, 38(5), 1102–1110. [Link](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/pde.14516)

- Kim, D. J., Makdisi, J., Regan, C., **Chen, P.-C.**, Chao, E., & Rotunda, A. M. (2020). Reconstruction of distal nasal defects using free cartilage batten grafting with secondary intention healing: A retrospective case series of 129 patients. *Dermatologic Surgery*, 47(1), 86–93. [Link](https://journals.lww.com/dermatologicsurgery/fulltext/2021/01000/reconstruction_of_distal_nasal_defects_using_free.24.aspx)
